name: research-smoke
description: Small source-backed QA suite for comparing search provider and model combinations in the Flue research harness.

cases.title: SimpleQA size and grading shape
cases.question: Using official SimpleQA material, answer: how many questions are in SimpleQA, and what answer property makes the questions easy to grade?
queries: OpenAI SimpleQA 4326 single indisputable answer easy to grade
Introducing SimpleQA 4326 questions easy to grade

expectedFacts.label: Questions have a single indisputable answer

cases.title: SimpleQA verification and error estimate
cases.question: Using the official SimpleQA page, answer: what random sample size did the third AI trainer verify, what match rate did they find, and what inherent dataset error rate did OpenAI estimate?
queries: OpenAI SimpleQA third AI trainer 1000 94.4 3% error rate
Introducing SimpleQA random sample 1000 matched 94.4 inherent error 3%

expectedFacts.label: Third trainer verified a random sample of 1,000 questions
Estimated inherent error rate was approximately 3%

cases.title: SimpleQA grading labels
cases.question: Using official SimpleQA material, answer: what are the three grading labels used by the prompted classifier, and what does the classifier compare?
queries: SimpleQA prompted classifier correct incorrect not attempted predicted ground truth
OpenAI SimpleQA grading correct incorrect not attempted

expectedFacts.label: The classifier compares predicted answer and ground-truth answer

cases.title: SimpleQA grading edge case
cases.question: Using the official SimpleQA grading examples, answer: for the World Cup example whose reference answer is Wout Weghorst, why is the response "Virgil van Dijk and Wout Weghorst" graded incorrect rather than correct?
queries: OpenAI SimpleQA Virgil van Dijk and Wout Weghorst incorrect
SimpleQA grading examples Virgil van Dijk Wout Weghorst incorrect

expectedFacts.label: The correct reference answer is Wout Weghorst
The extra Virgil van Dijk claim contradicts the reference answer

cases.title: BrowseComp purpose and size
cases.question: Using the official BrowseComp page, answer: what capability does BrowseComp measure, and how many challenging problems does it contain?
queries: OpenAI BrowseComp 1266 challenging problems browsing agents
BrowseComp measures hard-to-find information 1266

expectedFacts.label: It measures finding hard-to-find information by browsing

cases.title: SimpleQA versus BrowseComp comparison
cases.question: Using the official SimpleQA and BrowseComp pages, compare what each benchmark is meant to measure, and explain why BrowseComp was introduced even though SimpleQA exists.
queries: OpenAI SimpleQA measures factuality short fact seeking questions
OpenAI BrowseComp hard-to-find entangled information SimpleQA saturated browsing tools

expectedFacts.label: BrowseComp measures hard-to-find information retrieval by browsing
BrowseComp targets entangled information or browsing competition

cases.title: BrowseComp human verification difficulty
cases.question: Using the official BrowseComp page, answer: what share of problems did human trainers solve in the verification campaign, what was the agreement rate on solved problems, and what time limit was used before labeling a problem unsolvable?
queries: BrowseComp human trainers solved 29.2 agreement 86.4 two hours
OpenAI BrowseComp verification campaign 29.2 86.4 two hours

expectedFacts.label: The unsolvable cutoff was two hours

cases.title: BrowseComp verification arithmetic
cases.question: Using the official BrowseComp verification campaign numbers, answer: out of how many total problems, how many were labeled unsolvable, and what percentage was that?
queries: BrowseComp total problems verification campaign unsolvable 888 1255 70.8
OpenAI BrowseComp unsolvable 888 1255 70.8

expectedFacts.label: The verification campaign had 1,255 total problems

cases.title: BrowseComp baseline model accuracies
cases.question: Using the official BrowseComp page, report the listed accuracies for GPT-4o, GPT-4o with browsing, OpenAI o1, and Deep Research.
queries: BrowseComp GPT-4o 0.6 GPT-4o browsing 1.9 o1 9.9 Deep Research 51.5
OpenAI BrowseComp model accuracy table GPT-4o browsing deep research

cases.title: BrowseComp browsing delta
cases.question: Using the official BrowseComp model accuracy table, calculate the absolute improvement of GPT-4o with browsing over GPT-4o without browsing, in percentage points.
queries: BrowseComp GPT-4o 0.6 GPT-4o with browsing 1.9 improvement percentage points
OpenAI BrowseComp model accuracy GPT-4o browsing 1.9 0.6

expectedFacts.label: The absolute improvement was 1.3 percentage points

cases.title: BrowseComp best model and caveat
cases.question: Using the official BrowseComp page, identify the best-performing listed model in the table, its accuracy, and the caveat attached to that result.
queries: BrowseComp Deep Research 51.5 trained on data caveat
OpenAI BrowseComp Deep research 51.5 note trained on BrowseComp tasks

expectedFacts.label: Deep Research was the best-performing listed model
The caveat says Deep Research was trained on data teaching BrowseComp tasks

cases.title: BrowseComp task difficulty checks
cases.question: Using the official BrowseComp page, answer: what checks did trainers use to make questions sufficiently challenging?
queries: BrowseComp five simple searches solved more than 40 percent revised
OpenAI BrowseComp checks sufficiently challenging five searches 10 minutes 40%

expectedFacts.label: Existing models were checked and could not solve the question
Questions solved more than 40% of the time were revised

cases.title: GAIA public and retained answers
cases.question: Using the official GAIA publication page, answer: how many questions did GAIA devise, and how many answers were retained for the leaderboard?
queries: Meta GAIA benchmark 466 questions 300 answers retained leaderboard
GAIA benchmark devised 466 questions retained answers 300

expectedFacts.label: GAIA retained answers to 300 questions for the leaderboard

cases.title: GAIA capability requirements and performance gap
cases.question: Using the official GAIA publication page, answer: what abilities does GAIA require, and what human versus GPT-4-with-plugins scores are reported?
queries: Meta GAIA reasoning multi-modality web browsing tool-use human 92 GPT-4 plugins 15
GAIA benchmark human 92 GPT-4 plugins 15 fundamental abilities

cases.title: GAIA benchmark philosophy contrast
cases.question: Using the official GAIA publication page, explain how GAIA's benchmark philosophy differs from benchmarks that target tasks ever more difficult for humans.
queries: GAIA philosophy departs benchmarks ever more difficult for humans conceptually simple
Meta GAIA conceptually simple for humans challenging for AI benchmark philosophy

expectedFacts.label: GAIA questions are conceptually simple for humans
GAIA questions are challenging for advanced AIs
GAIA departs from targeting tasks ever more difficult for humans
GAIA emphasizes robustness similar to average human capability

cases.title: Flue local run command constraints
cases.question: Fetch https://flueframework.com/start.md, then answer: for local development, which Flue dev command targets are preferred, and what target does flue run support?
queries: Flue start.md flue dev target node cloudflare flue run target node
flueframework start.md flue run supports target node

expectedFacts.label: Local development prefers flue dev --target node
Local development prefers flue dev --target cloudflare
flue run only supports --target node
anyOf: flue run only supports target node

cases.title: Flue Cloudflare run negative control
cases.question: Fetch https://flueframework.com/start.md, then answer: is `flue run --target cloudflare` supported? If not, what should be used for local Cloudflare development and what target does `flue run` support?
queries: flue run --target cloudflare not supported flue dev --target cloudflare
Flue start.md flue run cloudflare not supported target node

expectedFacts.label: flue run --target cloudflare is not supported
Local Cloudflare development should use flue dev --target cloudflare

cases.title: Flue model selection guidance
cases.question: Fetch https://flueframework.com/start.md, then answer: which exact model IDs does the Flue start guide suggest, where should a different requested model be checked, and what should happen if the requested model is unavailable?
queries: flueframework start.md suggested model IDs claude sonnet opus gpt-5.5 kimi-k2.6 models.json
Flue start guide requested model unavailable ask before substituting

expectedFacts.label: Different models should be checked against models.json
Unavailable requested models should not be substituted without asking

cases.title: Flue workspace layout rules
cases.question: Fetch https://flueframework.com/start.md, then answer: what workspace layout should be used for a new or empty target directory, and what layout should be used for an existing non-empty directory?
queries: flueframework start.md new empty target directory root layout agents roles existing non-empty .flue
Flue start guide workspace layout root .flue existing non-empty

expectedFacts.label: New or empty directory uses root layout
